2606.09366.title: Is Text All You Need? Text as a Universal Information Bottleneck for Speech LLMs
2606.09366.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) provide a powerful reasoning backbone for speech understanding, but integrating continuous acoustic signals into a frozen LLM remains challenging. Existing speech-to-LLM interfaces typically operate at two extremes: either enforcing near-discrete token alignment, which benefits transcription but loses paralinguistic information, or learning unconstrained continuous representations, which can drift away from the LLM's input space and degrade autoregressive decoding. In this work, we propose Convex Gate (C-Gate), a speech-to-LLM bridge that constrains all speech representations to lie within the LLM's input embedding manifold with an architectural convex-hull constraint. Concretely, each frame is represented as a convex combination of token embeddings, ensuring compatibility with the pretrained LLM while preserving continuous expressivity. Across automatic speech recognition (ASR) and emotion recognition, C-Gate achieves strong joint performance, improving LibriSpeech WER by up to 48.7% relative while matching or exceeding single-task emotion accuracy. Beyond performance, our analysis reveals a key insight: information is not carried by discrete token identities, but by time-resolved trajectories in the embedding space. Causal interventions confirm that both the trajectory structure and alignment to the pretrained embedding manifold are critical for performance. These results suggest that geometry, rather than token discreteness, is the fundamental design factor in speech-to-LLM interfaces, and provide a controlled regime for studying multimodal integration in frozen LLMs. We release the checkpoint, per-sample outputs, mechanism dumps, and intervention suite for replication.
2606.09366.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）為語音理解提供了強大的推理基礎，但將連續的聲音信號整合進一個固定的LLM仍然具有挑戰性。現有的語音到LLM接口通常運作在兩個極端：要麼強制近離散的標記對齊，這有利於轉錄但失去了副語言信息；要麼學習不受限制的連續表示，這可能會偏離LLM的輸入空間並降低自回歸解碼的效果。在這項工作中，我們提出了Convex Gate（C-Gate），一個語音到LLM的橋樑，通過一個結構性的凸包約束，將所有語音表示限制在LLM的輸入嵌入流形內。具體而言，每一幀被表示為標記嵌入的凸組合，確保與預訓練LLM的兼容性，同時保留連續的表達能力。在自動語音識別（ASR）和情感識別中，C-Gate實現了強大的聯合性能，將LibriSpeech的WER提高了最多48.7%的相對值，同時達到或超過單任務情感準確度。除了性能，我們的分析揭示了一個關鍵見解：信息不是由離散的標記身份承載，而是由嵌入空間中的時間解析軌跡所攜帶。因果干預確認了軌跡結構和與預訓練嵌入流形的對齊對性能至關重要。這些結果表明，幾何而非標記的離散性是語音到LLM接口的基本設計因素，並為研究固定LLM中的多模態整合提供了一個受控的範疇。我們釋放了檢查點、每個樣本的輸出、機制轉儲和干預套件以供複製。
2606.09366.author: Ming-Hao Hsu et.al.
2606.09366.authors: Ming-Hao Hsu, Yuxuan Hu, Shujie Liu, Jinyu Li, Yan Lu, Zhizheng Wu
